title: Carbon pricing systems across Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Carbon pricing systems across Canada
As of May 15, 2026, the federal government is updating the headline price trajectory for all industrial carbon pricing systems in Canada. The headline price trajectory per tonne of CO2 equivalent emissions for all systems is now:
- Escalating 1.5% per year inflationary escalator starting in 2036 and ending at $140 in 2040
Taking into account feedback from provinces, territories, industry and other stakeholders, this trajectory advances climate ambition and grows our economy. By extending to 2040, the new trajectory provides the long-term certainty needed to support major decarbonization projects.
This new trajectory will be part of a full, updated federal benchmark that will be published later in 2026.
The Government of Canada will continue to work with our provincial and territorial partners to ensure all industrial carbon pricing systems are predictable, maximize cost-effective greenhouse gas emissions reductions, and support clean growth investments without adversely affecting competitiveness or leading to carbon leakage.
Canada has a long history as a leader on industrial carbon pricing, with provinces such as Alberta (2007), British Columbia (2008), and Quebec (2007, 2013) pioneering various types of industrial carbon pricing systems.
Since 2019, every province and territory has had an industrial carbon pricing system that meets the same minimum national stringency standards. Provinces and territories can design their own industrial carbon pricing system tailored to their needs as long as it meets federal minimum national stringency standards, also known as the federal ‘benchmark’.
- Minimum national stringency standards
Provinces and territories can design their own industrial carbon pricing system. If they choose not to or if their system that doesn’t meet the national standards, the federal industrial carbon pricing system is put in place.

## Carbon pollution pricing across Canada
Green : Provincial/Territorial system applies Blue : Federal Output-Based Pricing System (OBPS) for industry applies
Each jurisdiction has a label that indicates which system applies, as stated in the table below.
Newfoundland and Labrador
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Prince Edward Island
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Provincial cap-and-trade
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Provincial carbon pricing system for industry
Northwest Territories
Territorial carbon pricing system for industry
Note: Saskatchewan announced it was pausing its carbon pollution pricing system for industry effective April 1, 2025.

## Industrial carbon pricing across Canada
Below are links to web resources on each jurisdiction’s industrial carbon pricing system:
Please be advised that we are continuously updating the list of resources. Certain jurisdictions' systems are currently under development and their web resources may not be available at the moment.
